meta.name: DOCS Dimensional Obsessive-Compulsive Scale
meta.description: The DOCS is a 20-item self-report scale that assesses obsessive-compulsive symptom severity across four dimensions: contamination, responsibility for harm, unacceptable thoughts, and symmetry/ordering.
meta.author: Abramowitz JS, Deacon BJ, Olatunji BO, et al. (2010)
meta.reference: Abramowitz JS, Deacon BJ, Olatunji BO, et al. Assessment of obsessive-compulsive symptom dimensions. Psychol Assess. 2010;22(1):180-198.
meta.instruction: The following questions ask about four different types of concerns. Rate the degree to which you have experienced each aspect over the past month.

questions.text: How much time have you spent thinking about contamination (dirt, germs, chemicals) or feeling contaminated?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent contamination concerns?
If you had contamination thoughts but could not wash/clean, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by contamination concerns and washing/cleaning?
How difficult has it been to disregard contamination thoughts and refrain from washing/cleaning?
How much time have you spent thinking you might be responsible for something terrible happening?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent being responsible for harm?
If unable to check or get reassurance about potential harm, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by harm thoughts and checking/reassurance-seeking?
How difficult has it been to disregard harm-responsibility thoughts and refrain from checking?
How much time have you spent on unwanted intrusive thoughts (violent, sexual, immoral)?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent unwanted intrusive thoughts?
When you had unacceptable thoughts and could not perform a ritual, how distressed did you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by unacceptable thoughts and related rituals?
How difficult has it been to disregard unacceptable thoughts and refrain from rituals?
How much time have you spent on the need for symmetry, order, or balance?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent symmetry/order concerns?
If unable to arrange things to achieve symmetry, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by symmetry concerns and ordering?
How difficult has it been to disregard symmetry thoughts and refrain from ordering?

ranges.label: Minimal OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates minimal obsessive-compulsive symptoms within the normal range.
suggestions: No clinical concern at this time

ranges.label: Mild OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Mild obsessive-compulsive symptoms that may cause some distress but are manageable.
suggestions: Learn about OCD and intrusive thoughts

ranges.label: Moderate OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Moderate symptoms likely affecting your daily functioning.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from an OCD specialist
Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) is gold-standard treatment
Review subscale scores to identify problematic dimensions

ranges.label: Severe OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Severe symptoms causing significant impairment in daily life, work, and relationships.
suggestions: Please seek professional treatment as soon as possible
ERP therapy combined with medication may be most effective
OCD is a highly treatable condition

contamination.label: Moderate
contamination.description: Moderate contamination concerns affecting daily life.

contamination.label: Severe
contamination.description: Severe contamination obsessions and washing compulsions.

responsibility.label: Severe
responsibility.description: Severe harm obsessions and checking compulsions.

unacceptable_thoughts.label: Mild
unacceptable_thoughts.description: Mild distress from unwanted intrusive thoughts.

symmetry.label: Mild
symmetry.description: Mild need for symmetry or ordering.

symmetry.label: Moderate
symmetry.description: Moderate symmetry concerns affecting daily life.

symmetry.label: Severe
symmetry.description: Severe symmetry obsessions and ordering compulsions.
